strengths: TypeScript, Node.js, and React in production across multiple senior roles (Optilyz with AWS CDK, re:cinq) directly matching primary stack
Current role explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling — a rare, directly relevant differentiator
15+ years of fullstack breadth including high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby (millions of req/s), Kubernetes-deployed platforms, MongoDB and MySQL in production
concerns: NestJS is absent from the entire resume despite being a named primary stack requirement
Testing expertise centers on Selenium, D-lang tooling, and TDD methodology — Jest, Vitest, and Playwright are not mentioned
Kotlin is entirely missing; Java appears only in peripheral contexts (robotic arms, mobile device tooling) without enterprise backend depth
response.justification: The candidate shows strong alignment with core fullstack competencies: TypeScript and Node.js span her last three roles, React and AWS CDK were used in production at Optilyz, and her current re:cinq role directly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling — the most differentiating requirement in this posting. Scalability evidence is concrete (Dunnhumby microservices at millions of HTTP requests per second, GISCollective deployed on Kubernetes). The NestJS gap is real but not disqualifying given deep Node.js/TypeScript foundations; the absence of Jest, Vitest, and Playwright is a gap, though her testing culture is strong (authored a test runner, Fluent-Asserts library, taught TDD courses), suggesting fast framework ramp-up. Kotlin is missing entirely. On balance, the AI-agent experience and 15+ years of polyglot fullstack depth outweigh the framework-specific gaps for a mid-senior IC role.
